name: river_raid

comments.$000c: left_bank_table[0]: left river-bank X position for visible row 0. Entries $0C-$15 form a 10-row table the DLI compares against the current scanline X to detect bank collisions and to color the bank pixels.
comments.$0015: left_bank_table[9]: last entry of the 10-row left-bank position table at $0C-$15.
comments.$0018: Originally labelled `frame_counter` and assumed to be a monotonic per-frame counter. **EVIDENCE OF MISLABELLING:** sound.asm at $B21A explicitly stores `#$FF` into this byte from a non-VBI code path (`update_lives_display` entry, which is itself mislabelled). A monotonic counter wouldn't be force-set to $FF. Likely a state-transition timer rather than a frame counter. Verify with bridge watchpoint.
comments.$002b: Horizontal velocity accumulator. Incremented by 8 each main-loop frame while left/right joystick input is held (`lda $2B; clc; adc #$08; bcs skip; sta $2B` at $A45F). Capped at $F8 (carry overflow skips store). Reset to 0 on neutral input (`sta $2B` at $A46B). Used as the step for sub-pixel fractional accumulation in $58. Creates acceleration: jet starts slow (~1 pixel per 8 frames at velocity $08), ramps to near-pixel-per-frame at $F8. Verified via bridge.
comments.$0058: Horizontal fractional position. Each main-loop frame: `$58 += $2B` (right) or `$58 -= $2B` (left). Pixel movement (inc/dec player_sprite_x at $57) occurs only on 8-bit overflow/borrow. Reset to 0 on neutral input. Together with $2B implements the jet's inertia/acceleration curve. Verified via bridge.
comments.$0059: Vertical fractional position. Same sub-pixel accumulation system as $58 but for up/down movement. Used with a separate velocity variable in the vertical movement code at $A499+.
comments.$007d: Bullet fire timer. Set to $0F when TRIG0 is pressed (entities.asm fire detection at $A59D: `ldx #$0F; stx $7D`). Decremented each VBI frame at vbi.asm:$B387 (`dec $7D`). Drives the bullet sound effect (AUDF3 frequency computed from timer value) and possibly the bullet visual lifetime. When $7D reaches 0, bullet is expired.
comments.$007e: Bullet animation / sound counter. Decremented each VBI at $B399 (`dey; sty $7E`). Used to compute AUDF3 pitch and AUDC3 volume for the bullet flight sound effect. Reset alongside $7D during fire.
comments.$007f: M0 launch timer. Set to $09 at $A5E1 when the M0 fuel-depletion effect is triggered. Decremented each VBI at $B367 (`dec $7F`). While non-zero, drives a sound effect on AUDF2/AUDC2. The M0 motion loop at $A5CA only runs when fuel_level=0 or invuln_flags bit 7 is set.
comments.$001a: DLI-populated collision capture byte. Written by `sty $1A` at $B6A3 (river-zone DLI end) when M2PF bits 0-1 are set (via `lda HPOSP2` which reads M2PF). Holds the entity Y-index that was in scope when the capture fired; $FF = no hit. **M2 is the player bullet (corrected 2026-04-14)**, so this captures BULLET-vs-TERRAIN-BANK hits. Consumed by `entities.asm:$A58D`: `lda $1A; bpl loc_A589` — if hit, reset bullet via `handle_bullet_fire` with X=1 (at-rest).
comments.$001b: DLI-populated collision capture byte. Written by `sty $1B` at $B656 when P2PF bit 0 is set (via `lda HPOSM2` which reads P2PF). **This is the JET-VS-TERRAIN capture** — PF0 is the bank/bridge color, so a P2PF bit 0 hit means the player's jet touched a bank or bridge. Holds the entity slot index active during the collision; $FF = no hit. Consumed by entities.asm `check_entity_collision` at $A42E (`ldx $1B; bmi loc_A43F`).
comments.$001c: DLI-populated collision capture byte. Written by `sty $1C` at $B5F1 (inside the WSYNC loop) and $B727 (river-zone post) when M2PL bit 3 is set — i.e. M2 hit P3 (the enemy multiplexer). **M2 is the player bullet (corrected 2026-04-14)**, so this captures BULLET-vs-ENEMY hits; the M2 bitmap stripe at the bullet's moving Y overlaps P3 when the bullet reaches an enemy. Consumed by `entities.asm:$A4B6` (`ldx $1C`). HPOSM2 = jet_sprite_x + 4 at $B539, so the bullet always fires from 4 px right of the jet centre. Previously described as a 'narrow jet hit probe' — that framing was wrong because M2's bitmap only exists in the bullet's active scanlines, not at the jet's Y row.
comments.$001d: DLI-populated collision capture byte. Written by `sty $1D` at $B614 and $B72B when P2PL bit 3 is set (after `lda P2PL; lsr; and #$04`). **This is the WIDE JET-VS-ENEMY capture** — P2 is the jet, P3 is enemy. Consumed by entities.asm `check_entity_collision` at $A3E9 (`ldx $1D`). Distinct from $1C (which uses M2 as a narrower probe).
comments.$001e: DLI-populated collision capture byte. Written by `sty $1E` at $B60F and $B723 when P2PL bit 0 is set — i.e. P2 (jet) vs P0. DORMANT in the base game because P0 has no bitmap. Would fire if anything ever put a bitmap in P0. Consumed at $A424 (`ldx $1E; bmi loc_A42E`), paired with a `collision_result >= $11` check — look there to understand the consumer's intent.
comments.$001f: DLI-populated collision capture byte. Written by `sty $1F` at $B5EA (WSYNC loop) and $B731 when P3PL bit 0 is set — i.e. P3 (enemy) vs P0. DORMANT in the base game because P0 has no bitmap. Consumed at $A4B0 (`ldx $1F`).
comments.$0020: DLI-populated collision capture byte. Written by `sty $20` at $B64A and $B73D when P1PF bit 1 is set (`lda HPOSM1` reads P1PF). DORMANT — P1 has no bitmap. Consumed at $A4B9 (`ldx $1F; bpl handle_extra_life` then the fall-through reads $20 context). Also referenced as `bit $20` in state_machine.asm.
comments.$0021: DLI-populated collision capture byte. Written by `sty $21` at $B638 and $B713 when M0PL bit 2 is set (`lda SIZEP0` reads M0PL, `and #$04`) — i.e. M0 hit P2 (jet). NOT DORMANT: M0 is actively used (see $00C1). When the M0 dot is overlapping the jet, this capture fires. Consumed at $A42E (`ldx $21; bpl handle_extra_life`).
comments.$0022: DLI-populated collision capture byte. Written by `sty $22` at $B641 and $B71C when M0PF bits 0-1 are set (`lda HPOSP0` reads M0PF, `and #$03`). **This is the M0-VS-PLAYFIELD capture** — M0 touched PF0 or PF1 (bank/bridge/detail). Consumed at $A5FD in bullets_terrain.asm (`ldx $22; bpl terrain_gen_advance`) where it stops the M0 motion loop on playfield impact. Strong evidence that M0 is a projectile-like element, not a decoration.
comments.$0024: Unidentified zero-page variable written by the attract handler at $A1BF (ldy #$00; sty $24) and read by state_dispatch at $A1CE (ldx $24; bne loc_A1DC). Runtime values observed: $00 / $48 / $80 during attract, $00 in active player mode — inconsistent with a 0/1/2/3 state machine. The older 'game_state: 0=attract 1=play 2=dying 3=starting' interpretation was wrong (unverifiable in live gameplay). Real play-state variable not yet identified.
comments.$0027: Status-bar sprite HPOS (P3). Written to HPOSP3 at $B752 in the status-bar DLI; also driven from VBI timing code around $B4XX. Not bullet state — the old 'bullet_x' label was wrong.
comments.$0028: start_pressed: latched CONSOL START bit, checked in attract mode to leave state 0 and enter state 3 (game starting).
comments.$0029: invuln_flags: post-death invulnerability/respawn flags while game_state=2.
comments.$002a: LABEL IS MISLEADING. This is a countdown timer, not a visible life counter. Decremented every 8 frames by entity_dispatch tail at $A2F5-$A310 (`lda frame_counter; and #$07; bne skip; dex`); when the countdown expires it sets `invuln_flags |= $80` and jumps to the `player_death` hook at $A688. Reset to $25 (37) by the death hook region at $A6D5 (`ldx #$25; stx lives_count`) — implausible as a lives count. Reset to $00 on START press at $A6ED (bullets_terrain path). Likely a fuel-starvation / stuck-state watchdog timer. The real visible lives counter is somewhere else and has not been located. Name retained for backward compatibility; do not reason from the name.
comments.$002e: bridges_crossed: bridge counter. Tracks overall level progression; every bridge crossing awards bonus points and may raise enemy density. Companion to $62.
comments.$0032: **MISLABELED:** Previously called 'fuel_drain_ctr'. Decremented at $AE94 by `decrement_fuel`. NOT actually the fuel drain counter — the real fuel drain counter is at $5C. The role of $32 (and the relationship to $5C/$5B) is currently unverified. Treat as opaque pending re-analysis.
comments.$0038: **MISLABELED:** Previously called 'fuel_state'. Bit 7 is set when bullet hits a high-type entity (entities.asm $A517: ora #$80; sta $38). Previous claim 'increments $76 each frame' was wrong on two counts: (1) $76 is not fuel ($5B is); (2) no code increments fuel in response to $38 bit 7. The actual refuel mechanism is the jet body collision path at $A40A which increments $5B unconditionally. Role of $38 is currently TBD beyond 'sometimes set when bullet hits a depot'.
comments.$0039: Written to HPOSP1 at $B54F in the playfield DLI each frame. However the P1 DMA buffer ($0D00-$0DFF) is never populated with a bitmap — verified via bridge write-watches and runtime DMA sweep (0 non-zero bytes across thousands of frames including active player mode with fire held). **The actual bullet sprite is M2** (packed missile DMA), drawn at $B223 which writes $20 (M2 bit 5) to $0B01+$56 .. $0B08+$56 (`sound.asm:loc_B22E`). HPOSM2 = player_sprite_x + 4 at DLI $B539. As `$56` decrements each frame via `handle_bullet_fire`, the bullet stripe rises up the screen. `$1A` captures M2 vs playfield (bullet-vs-terrain-bank reset). M1 is the **fuel bar** (not the bullet) — HPOSM1 = ($5B>>3)+$5C (literal) at $B52C drives M1's X from fuel level, and the stripe at $0BCC-$0BD5 is in the status-panel Y range. The slow inc/dec at $A25B/$A25F oscillates $39 independent of fire input. True role of $39 is not yet characterised; `bullet_x` is a legacy label and the HPOSP1 write appears to be vestigial.
comments.$003a: scroll_position: playfield scroll accumulator reset to $0C at $AE86 alongside the fuel/terrain reset path.
comments.$003b: Fuel-gauge row index. Reset to 0 at $A671/$AF05, read at $AF0F where it's ASL'd and used to index the fuel_gauge_ptrs table at $BAB7 (2-byte pointers) before blitting the next gauge row. Not bullet state — the old 'bullet_state' label was wrong.
comments.$003c: Terrain scroll accumulator (low byte of 16-bit pair with $3D). Updated by terrain_bank_update at $A652 via ADC/SBC with $3E/$3F. Also written to HPOSP0 at $B559 as the playfield DLI's P0 baseline. Observed = 0 during gameplay, so P0 is hidden at X=0 in the playfield zone — P0 has no active visual role there.
comments.$003d: Terrain scroll accumulator (high byte of 16-bit pair with $3C). Updated together with $3C at $A652.
comments.$003e: Terrain bank delta low - added/subtracted into $3C/$3D by terrain_bank_update at $A652.
comments.$003f: Terrain bank delta high - added/subtracted into $3C/$3D by terrain_bank_update at $A652.
comments.$0041: sound_flags: sound engine request flags, reset to $FF in the fuel/terrain reset path at $AE86.
comments.$0050: collision_result: per-frame collision result flag, clamped in terrain_bank_update at $A652 and reset to $FF at $AE86.
comments.$0052: Player horizontal position, river-relative (range roughly $10-$30). Converted to absolute HPOS as player_sprite_x = $52 + $26 + river_width_R at $A4E4. Joystick input updates player_sprite_x directly (inc/dec at $A47F/$A591); $52 tracks separately for river-space logic.
comments.$0053: river_width_L: current river left edge parameter. Together with $54 defines the usable river channel for the player.
comments.$0054: river_width_R: current river right edge parameter. Used in the player_sprite_x conversion at $A4E4.
comments.$0057: Player jet horizontal position. Written to HPOSP2 at $B534 in the playfield DLI — P2 is the jet. Joystick left/right inc/dec this directly at $A47F/$A591. VERIFIED via bridge runtime snapshot: HPOSP2 == $57 in gameplay state.
comments.$005a: section_type: current river section style (straight, narrow, winding). Drives terrain generation.
comments.$005d: terrain_density: enemy density for the current terrain section. Feeds the spawner at $B0C7 - higher values spawn more frequently.
comments.$005f: spawn_cooldown: frames-until-next-spawn counter used by the entity spawner at $B0C7.
comments.$0062: bridge counter companion to $2E. Both are bumped on bridge crossings to drive scoring and difficulty ramps.
comments.$0063: score_high: BCD score high byte (hundred-thousands / millions). Updated by the SED/ADC routine at $A547.
comments.$0064: score_mid: BCD score mid byte (thousands / ten-thousands). Updated at $A547.
comments.$0065: score_low: BCD score low byte (ones / tens / hundreds). Updated at $A547.
comments.$006a: extra_life_thresh: score threshold for the next extra-life award. Checked after each scoring event.
comments.$0076: **LABEL CORRECTED 2026-04-14:** Previously called `fuel_level`. NOT fuel. Verified via Altirra cheat search: freezing $76 has no effect on fuel; freezing $5B halts fuel drain. Real fuel level is at $5B. The role of $76 (and the $B200 STA $76 with #$FE) is currently unknown; only 5 writers exist in ROM ($A178 init=$FF, $A339 set=0, $A358/$A36A dec, $B200 reset=$FE/$FF). Treat as opaque counter pending further analysis.
comments.$005b: **REAL FUEL LEVEL.** Verified 2026-04-14 via Altirra cheat search (freeze $5B → fuel stops draining). Range $FF=full to $00=empty. Companion drain counter at $5C. Decremented at $A451 (DEC $5B) when $5C - $20 borrows. Incremented at $A418 (STA $5B after ADC #$01 at $A40A) while jet body ($1D) overlaps an entity of type > $0E (fuel depots are types $0F+). When $5B reaches 0, next drain triggers death sound via JMP $A43A. Previous label `m1_x_shift_src` was wrong.
comments.$005c: Fuel drain counter (real). Companion to $5B. Each frame at $A43F: A = $5C - $20; if no borrow (BCS $A453), store back. If borrow, decrement $5B (fuel) and store new $5C. Reset to $FF at $A414 when refuel overflow occurs (fuel hits $FF). Previous label `m1_x_base` was wrong.
comments.$0077: player_number: active player (0 or 1) for two-player alternation. Selects the joystick nibble at $A312 and the COLPM2 value at $B510 ($8E vs $1E).
comments.$007a: engine_sound_param_0: engine-hum frequency/control byte read by the VBI sound engine at $B2A5.
comments.$007b: engine_sound_param_1: engine-hum frequency/control byte read by the VBI sound engine at $B2A5.
comments.$00a5: terrain_seed_lo: low byte of the terrain PRNG state. Advanced by $A5B7; drives river-bank positions and obstacle layout.
comments.$00a6: terrain_seed_hi: high byte of the terrain PRNG state. Advanced by $A5B7.
comments.$00c1: M0 sprite state byte. Two simultaneous roles: (1) **HPOSM0 source** — read in the playfield DLI at $B561 (`lda $C1; sta HPOSM0`) every frame, so this byte is M0's screen X position. (2) **M0 visibility gate** — read in frame_sync.asm (around $AEF4) where the missile-DMA byte writer at `$0B00+frame_ctr_copy` does `lda $0B00,X; and #$FC; ldy $C1; beq skip; ora #$03; sta $0B00,X` — setting M0 bits only when $C1 is non-zero. Updated by the motion loop in bullets_terrain.asm at $A5CA-$A607 together with $C0 (direction) and $22 (M0 vs PF hit-stop). **CORRECTED (2026-04-13):** M0 has dual role: (1) fuel bar indicator during normal play — the code at $AEC8-$AEF9 draws M0 bits and cycles COLPM0 through yellow-green, with $C1 as the bar X position (moves left as fuel drains); (2) death-effect dot when fuel=0 — the motion loop at $A5CA only runs at fuel=0/invuln. Originally labelled `terrain_draw_flag`; better name: `m0_x_or_alive`.
comments.$00c0: Direction byte for the M0 motion loop in bullets_terrain.asm. Set in the entry path at $A5F8 to either $FE (move left, decrement) or $02 (move right, increment) based on a bit-7 test of an entity flag at $42,X. Each frame the motion loop adds $C0 to $C1 to step M0's X position, then stops when $22 indicates an M0-vs-playfield hit. Originally unlabelled.
comments.$00c6: sound_timer: explosion sound countdown. When non-zero the VBI at $B2A5 plays a noise burst on channel 1 (AUDF1=$40, AUDC1=$AF) and decrements the timer.
comments.$02e2: INITAD vector -> $0400. Loaded from segment 2 so DOS jumps into the init code immediately after loading.
comments.$0400: Init code entry (segment 1). Sets CASINI ($02/$03) to $4086, JSRs the relocator at $4086, then loops via JMP ($000C) / JMP ($000A). Reached through INITAD ($02E2/$02E3 = $0400).
comments.$0500: entity_type[0..5]: 6-slot entity-type table. **Type ranges corrected 2026-04-14:** 0=empty, 1-6=enemies (ships/helicopters/jets handled by various paths), 7-$0D=enemies that kill jet on $1D body collision (handled at $A3FC: play_score_sound + destroy), $0E=bridge ($A3FC after inc bridges_crossed), $0F+=fuel depots (handled at $A40A: ADC #$01 / STA $5B = increment fuel by 1 per frame). Previous claim '7=fuel depot, $0C=balloon' was wrong. Base of the parallel entity arrays at $0500-$0526.
comments.$0506: entity_anim[0..4]: 5-slot entity animation-frame table. Parallel to entity_type at $0500.
comments.$050b: entity_shape[0..5]: 6-slot entity shape/sprite-table-index table. Parallel to entity_type at $0500.
comments.$0516: entity_xpos[0..5]: 6-slot entity horizontal-position table. Compared against bullet_x at $A500/$A535 for bullet-hit detection.
comments.$0521: entity_size[0..5]: 6-slot entity SIZEP width table. Read by the DLI to set per-scanline SIZEP3 during sprite multiplexing.
comments.$0600: Missile PMG graphics base ($0600-$06BF). PMBASE is set to $06 at $D407, so player/missile hardware reads its shapes from here.
comments.$0700: Player 0 PMG bitmap ($0700-$07CF): the player's jet shape.
comments.$0800: Player 1 PMG bitmap ($0800-$08CF): the player's bullet column.
comments.$0818: Bullet P1 bitmap column range ($0818-$0887). The bullet's HPOS ($39) stays constant; upward motion is simulated by shifting which bytes in this column are non-zero.
comments.$0900: Players 2-3 PMG bitmap ($0900-$09CF): enemies and fuel depots, repositioned per-scanline by the DLI at $B567 to multiplex up to 6 on-screen entities across only 2 hardware players.
comments.$2000: Main river terrain bitmap start ($2000-$3C7F). ANTIC mode 14, ~160 scanlines. LMS'd from the display list at $3F0C.
comments.$3d80: Status-bar screen RAM start ($3D80-$3EFF): score, lives, fuel gauge - 9 scanlines of ANTIC mode 14. LMS'd from the display list at $3F03.
comments.$3f00: Display list base. Template copied from $B776 at cold start. Starts with 3x blank-8-line instructions (24 blank scanlines), then LMS into $3D80 (status), then LMS into $2000 (playfield), ending with JVB -> $3F00 at $3FB5.
comments.$3f01: Display list DLI trigger: blank-8 opcode with DLI bit set, firing the DLI at $B500 at the top of the playfield.
comments.$3f03: Display list LMS mode 14 -> $3D80: status/score area (9 scanlines).
comments.$3f0c: Display list LMS mode 14 -> $2000: main river playfield (~160 scanlines).
comments.$3fb5: Display list JVB -> $3F00: jump-and-wait-for-VBI, restarts the display list each frame.
comments.$4080: Bootstrap (pre-relocation) segment 0 base. Carries the relocator at $4086 and the game image at $4100-$60FF.
comments.$4086: Relocator: (1) copies 32 pages ($2000 bytes) from $4100 to $A000, (2) zero-fills $0400-$3EFF to clear the init code and lower RAM, (3) JSR through JMP ($BFFE) to run IRQ setup, (4) JMP ($BFFA) to the cold start at $A000.
comments.$a000: cold_start: disable NMI, IRQ, GRACTL, DMACTL; zero all HW regs. Entry point jumped to via ($BFFA) after the relocator at $4086 finishes.
comments.$a037: JSR $B298 - read console switches, check for cartridge/START at startup.
comments.$a04e: Zero-fill RAM $00 through $3FFF before setting up the display list.
comments.$a05d: Copy display list template from $B776 to $3F00.
comments.$a066: Set up display list pointers (DLISTL/DLISTH -> $3F00) after copying the template.
comments.$a06a: Detect PAL/NTSC from $D014 bit 3; store in pal_flag.
comments.$a15d: State 3: begin new game — clear vars, init lives/score/fuel, enter gameplay.
comments.$a197: Attract mode / title handler: wait for VCOUNT >= $78, poll CONSOL/joystick, fall through to main loop at $AE99.
comments.$a19e: Read joystick port A (PORTA $D300) during attract mode.
comments.$a1ae: Read CONSOL ($D01F): bit0=START, bit1=SELECT, bit2=OPTION. START leaves attract into state 3.
comments.$a1ce: State machine dispatch: LDX game_state ($24); BNE to $A1DC for non-attract states, otherwise fall through into attract handling and JMP $AE99.
comments.$a1d9: Dispatch exit: JMP $AE99 - hand control to the main frame loop after per-frame state work.
comments.$a1dc: Non-attract dispatch tail: CPX #$02 etc., routing to $A271 (gameplay), $A3E9 (dying), $A15D (starting).
comments.$a271: State 1 - Entity update loop: iterate 6 entity slots at $0500, move each by type, spawn new ones via $B0C7, check ranges.
comments.$a312: Input handling: LDA PORTA, select nibble by player_number ($77) - low nibble for player 1, high nibble LSR'd x4 for player 2 - store direction in $19.
comments.$a325: Player 1 joystick branch: use PORTA low nibble directly.
comments.$a3e9: check_entity_collision: the actual collision-to-effect dispatcher. Reads the DLI-populated collision-capture bytes at $1A-$22 (which hold entity Y-indices, not simple flags) and branches per category. Specifically: `ldx $1D` (jet1 wide hit vs enemy) at $A3E9, `ldx $1E` (P2 vs P0, dormant) at $A424, `ldx $21` (M0 vs P2) at $A42E, `ldx $1B` (jet1 vs PF0 = bank/bridge = TERRAIN DEATH) at $A435, `ldx $1C` (M2 vs P3 = narrow jet hit probe vs enemy) at $A4B6, `ldx $1F` (P3 vs P0, dormant) at $A4C1. Each branch triggers scoring, enemy destruction, or player death depending on which capture fired. The old comment mislabeled this as 'State 2 - player dying' — it is in fact called from the main state-1 path at $A33F when `fuel_level == 0`, AND from elsewhere; needs full call-site audit.
comments.$a4e0: Entity hit processing: set destruction animation, compute BCD score from $BB0B[type], schedule sound.
comments.$a4e4: player_sprite_x = player_x ($52) + $26 + river_width_R ($54) - converts river-relative to absolute HPOS, stored at $57.
comments.$a500: Bullet-vs-entity range check (low slot): compares bullet_x ($39) against entity_xpos. Hit sets fuel_state bit 7 ($38 |= $80) and schedules score/explosion.
comments.$a535: Bullet-vs-entity range check (high slot): paired with $A500 for the upper entity slots.
comments.$a547: Score update: SED + ADC BCD addition of the entity's score-table entry at $BB0B into $63/$64/$65.
comments.$a5aa: handle_bullet_fire / M0 motion entry. Saves X, calls the M-DMA byte-clear helper at $B221, stores result into $56 (bullet_column). Then this routine continues into bullets_terrain.asm proper. The motion loop at $A5CA-$A607 reads `$C1` (current M0 X) and `$22` (M0 vs PF hit-stop) to either advance M0's X position by `$C0` (when an entity slot $51 of type $0A or $0B is being processed and the playfield is clear) or terminate the bullet on a playfield collision. This strongly suggests M0 is a bullet/projectile element (likely the player's in-flight bullet, with M1 being the muzzle flash template), but the exact semantic of `$51` (entity slot index) and entity types $0A/$0B needs verification. Also reads `fuel_level` and `invuln_flags` as gates. Originally labelled `handle_bullet_fire` but it does much more than handle fire press; rename candidates: `process_m0_motion`, `bullet_motion_advance`.
comments.$a5b7: Advance terrain PRNG ($A5/$A6), compute new river bank positions for the next row.
comments.$a652: Terrain bank update: 16-bit accumulation into $3C/$3D via ADC/SBC with $3E/$3F. Also clamps collision_result ($50) and clears fuel_gauge_idx ($3B = 0).
comments.$a688: player_death: NOT THE REAL DEATH HANDLER. This is a 5-instruction hook: `lda $09; and #$01; sta player_number; ldx #$79; jmp state_start_game`. It toggles `player_number` from bit 0 of `$09` and jumps into `state_start_game` at $A15D (which clears RAM from $79 upward via the bpl loop at $A15F-$A164). The actual death processing — explosion animation, lives decrement, level reset, sound triggering — lives elsewhere: partially in `state_dying_update` at $A7FB (state 2 of `var_0024`), partially in routines reached from `check_entity_collision` at $A3E9, and partially in the collision-capture variables at $1A-$22 written by the DLI. To trace the real death sequence, watchpoint the byte that decrements when the on-screen lives icon visibly disappears — that byte is the entry point.
comments.$a701: Main loop tail: fuel management, jump back to frame sync.
comments.$ae85: End of entity update loop: after all 6 slots have been processed, fall through to collision/fuel handling.
comments.$ae86: fuel_and_continue tail: stores computed bullet_x ($39 = table + player_x + offsets), writes fuel_state ($38 = Y), resets scroll_position ($3A = $0C), collision_result ($50 = $FF), sound_flags ($41 = $FF), then falls into decrement_fuel.
comments.$ae90: Fuel management entry: check fuel_state ($38) and fuel_level ($76) before decrementing the drain counter.
comments.$ae94: DEC fuel_drain_ctr ($32); on wrap, the main loop decrements fuel_level ($76). JMP back to main_loop_continue.
comments.$ae99: === MAIN FRAME SYNC === wait for VCOUNT < $50 (top of visible area). Entered from the state dispatch at $A1D9 once per frame.
comments.$aea0: JSR frame_update ($AFD0) - advance terrain, prepare display for the next frame.
comments.$aeb5: Terrain scroll start: advance the terrain bank tables and prepare to blit a new row at the bottom of the playfield.
comments.$af00: Redraw fuel gauge rows: reset fuel_gauge_idx ($3B=0), then loop reading $BAB7,X pointer pairs and blitting each row via copy_row_data.
comments.$af5a: Terrain scroll end: falls through into per-frame housekeeping before the entity loop.
comments.$afd0: Frame update: scroll terrain, copy new row data to screen, advance display-list LMS pointers.
comments.$aff4: Fill terrain row: write pattern $55 to a mode-14 bitmap line (used to prefill newly-exposed river rows).
comments.$b00f: Blit Y+1 bytes from (src_ptr) to (dst_ptr) - general row copy helper.
comments.$b05c: check_terrain_collision: LABEL IS WRONG. Entry point into the shared digit-rendering helper at $B086. Sets `$B1 = $90`, X = $61, Y = $23 (immediate values used as render parameters), then `lda #$02; bne loc_B086` falls through into the nibble-extraction code. Renders some category of digits into screen RAM at `$1000+Y`. Not a collision check. See $B07C for the other entry points and $B086 for the actual nibble loop.
comments.$b064: Entry point into the digit-rendering helper. Loads `lda #$02; bne loc_B086`. One of several entry points (also $B05C, $B068, $B06E, $B07C) that set up different X/Y/$B1 values before falling into the shared nibble-extraction loop at $B086.
comments.$b068: Entry point into the digit-rendering helper. Sets `lda #$50; ldy #$0C; bne loc_B080`. One of several entry points into the shared digit renderer.
comments.$b06e: Entry point into the digit-rendering helper. Reads `$09`, masks `#$0E`, ASLs and ADCs `#$80` to compute X (a screen RAM offset based on player number), then `ldy #$0C; bne loc_B082` to enter the renderer. Probably the per-player score renderer.
comments.$b07c: process_collision_results: LABEL IS WRONG. This routine does NOT process collision flags. It is one of several entry points into a shared **digit-rendering helper** that sets up X/Y/$B1/$B2 from immediate values, then falls through to the nibble-extraction loop at $B08C which reads `temp_ptr_lo,X` (a BCD byte), splits into high and low nibbles, and writes them to screen RAM at `$1000+Y`. Other entry points: $B05C, $B064, $B068, $B06E. They render score/fuel/lives digits into the status bar. The actual collision-to-effect mapping lives in `check_entity_collision` at $A3E9 (entities.asm).
comments.$b0c7: Entity spawner: find empty entity slot, select type from $BB24 transition table, init position/shape, reset spawn_cooldown ($5F).
comments.$b0d8: Per-entity movement dispatch: stationary / oscillate / track player - depends on entity_type at $0500.
comments.$b183: Redraw fuel gauge bars in status screen RAM at $3D80+.
comments.$b1c0: play_score_sound: LABEL IS WRONG. The routine touches NO POKEY audio register. It saves Y/A into $7B/$7A, then branches based on `$4C` and `$23` state flags. Eventually it can call `update_score_display` ($B194) and the M-DMA byte-clear helper at $B221, then falls into $B200 which stores `$FE → fuel_level ($76)` and `$00 → lives_count ($2A)`. Actual purpose is unverified — likely a state-transition / reset helper related to the score display or the death sequence, possibly the routine that arms a sound effect via a separate sound queue rather than poking AUDF/AUDC directly. Needs bridge trace before relying on the name.
comments.$b21a: update_lives_display: LABEL IS WRONG. The routine stores `$FF → frame_counter ($18)`, then `tax; bne loc_B200` to jump into the same $B200 block that `play_score_sound` falls into. It does NOT update any visible lives counter on screen. Whatever it actually does, it ends with the same fuel_level/lives_count store sequence as $B1C0. Verify with bridge before using the name.
comments.$b221: M-DMA byte-clear helper. Called from various paths. Loads A=$00 and falls into $B223. Originally mislabelled `clear_status_line`/`write_status_text` — it does not touch status RAM. It clears 8 consecutive bytes of the missile DMA buffer at `$0B01+X` through `$0B08+X` (X comes from $56 with bounds clamping). Clearing all bit slices wipes M0/M1/M2/M3 in that window, which is consistent with a bullet-trail clearer that erases the previous frame's bullet position before redraw.
comments.$b223: Continuation of the M-DMA byte-clear helper at $B221. The actual `sta $0B01,X` ... `sta $0B08,X` block. Originally mislabelled `write_status_text` — it does not write any text or status data; it stores zero bytes (the A from $B221) into 8 consecutive missile DMA bytes.
comments.$b298: Read CONSOL switches ($D01F), return decoded START/SELECT/OPTION in A.
comments.$b2a5: === VBI HANDLER === sound engine — engine hum (AUDF1/AUDC1), explosion burst driven by $C6, score/refuel effects on channels 2-3, silence when $24 is in attract/dying.
comments.$b3d8: VBI continuation: game timers, color animation, end of per-frame housekeeping before exiting via XITVBV.
comments.$b48d: Color tables: per-entity-type color palettes (68 bytes through $B4D0). Indexed by entity type when setting COLPM during the DLI.
comments.$b4d1: Terrain row pointer table: pairs of addresses (48 bytes through $B4FF) feeding the terrain row copier.
comments.$b500: === DLI HANDLER === save regs, branch by VCOUNT at $B509: VCOUNT<$3F -> playfield kernel at $B510, else status-bar handler at $B72A.
comments.$b509: DLI dispatch: compares current VCOUNT to $3F and branches to the playfield or status-bar kernel accordingly.
comments.$b510: === DLI PLAYFIELD KERNEL === Baseline stores for the playfield zone: clears collision-capture bytes $1A-$22 to $FF; computes HPOSM1 = ($5B>>3)+$5C at $B52C (M1 = bullet, at-rest sub-position); HPOSP2 = player_sprite_x ($57, the jet) at $B534; HPOSM2 = P2+4 at $B539 (M2 is the JET HIT PROBE — a narrower sprite tracking the jet at +4 px offset, NOT a dead store; the M2 vs P3 collision is captured into $1C); COLPM1 conditional store at $B541 ($00 above the river, $1E in the river zone via $B697); HPOSP1 = bullet_x ($39) at $B54F (this IS a dead store today — P1's DMA buffer is never populated, verified — but M1 inherits its color from COLPM1 so the COLPM1 manipulation is meaningful); HPOSM0 = $C1 at $B561 (M0 X position — NOT a dead store; M0 is actively used by frame_sync.asm:52-59 which writes M0 bits at $0B00+frame_ctr_copy gated by $C1, see $00C1); HPOSP0 = $3C at $B559 (terrain scroll low byte = 0 in gameplay, so P0 is at X=0 = effectively hidden — P0 is genuinely unused). Falls into WSYNC loop at $B567 which multiplexes enemies on P3. **Important:** the playfield DLI does more per-line work than the original analysis credited — specifically HPOSM2 positioning and the M0 X store. Cycle budget for any new stores must be measured.
comments.$b534: DLI baseline: HPOSP2 = player_sprite_x ($57). The jet is on P2. Verified: moving $57 moves the jet sprite.
comments.$b54d: DLI baseline: HPOSP1 = bullet_x ($39) - positions the bullet (P1) for the playfield zone.
comments.$b557: DLI baseline: HPOSP0 = $3C (terrain scroll low byte) - P0 inherits its X from the terrain scroll accumulator. $3C is observed = 0 during gameplay so this hides P0 at X=0. Genuinely dead store for the active gameplay state, but is reachable.
comments.$b561: DLI baseline: HPOSM0 = $C1. Sets M0's screen X each frame from the byte at $00C1. NOT A DEAD STORE — M0 has bits actively written into $0B00+frame_ctr_copy by frame_sync.asm:52-59 (gated by $C1 != 0), so M0 is a visible (or at least DMA-active) sprite. See $00C1 for the dual-role state byte and the related motion loop in bullets_terrain.asm.
comments.$b539: DLI baseline: HPOSM2 = player_sprite_x + 4. Positions M2 four pixels right of the jet. M2 is a narrow secondary hit probe for the jet — M2 vs P3 collisions are captured into $1C and consumed by entities.asm at $A4B6, providing a narrower (more forgiving) collision hitbox than P2's full 16-pixel sprite. The M2 bit slice of the missile DMA buffer must have bits set somewhere for this to work; the writer has not been located in the static analysis but the design strongly implies it exists. Investigate via runtime DMA sweep over `$0B00-$0BFF` bits 4-5.
comments.$b567: DLI WSYNC loop: per-scanline entity multiplexer (hottest code). Only HPOSP3/COLPM3/SIZEP3 change per scanline — HPOSP0/1/2 keep their $B510 baselines.
comments.$b5ea: DLI collision read inside the WSYNC loop: after each entity zone, reads M2PL bit 3 (M2 hit P3 = jet hit probe vs enemy) and stores Y into $1C, then reads P3PL bit 0 (P3 vs P0 = enemy vs P0, dormant) and stores Y into $1F. The original comment said 'bullet-hit-enemy (M2PL bit 3)' but M2 is the jet hit probe, not the bullet — the bullet is M1 (and possibly M0). M2PL bit 3 means 'something on M2 touched something on P3' which under the corrected sprite map is jet-hit-probe-vs-enemy.
comments.$b5f1: DLI WSYNC-loop collision tail: stores into the per-entity collision-capture bytes ($1C, $1F) before continuing the WSYNC loop. See $00C1 family of comments for the meaning of each capture byte.
comments.$b60b: DLI end-of-zone collision capture block. Reads several collision registers in sequence and stores entity Y-indices into $1A-$22 when bits are set. Specifically (in order): `lda P2PL` then captures bit 0 → $1E (P2 vs P0, dormant) and bit 3 → $1D (P2 vs P3 = jet wide vs enemy); `lda SIZEP0` (= M0PL) bit 2 → $21 (M0 vs P2 = M0 hit jet); `lda HPOSP0` (= M0PF) bits 0-1 → $22 (M0 vs PF0-1 = M0 vs bank/bridge); `lda HPOSM1` (= P1PF) bit 1 → $20 (dormant, P1 unused); `lda HPOSM2` (= P2PF) bit 0 → $1B (jet vs PF0 = TERRAIN DEATH); `lda HPOSP2` (= M2PF) bits 0-1 → $1A (M2 vs PF, dormant). Then `sta HITCLR` ($D01E) clears the hardware collision registers. Note: the hardware addresses $D000-$D00F have dual write (HPOS/SIZE/GRAF) and read (collision) semantics, which is why the DLI uses `lda HPOSP0` etc to read collisions.
comments.$b681: DLI river-zone: COLPM1 = $1E (P1 becomes visible here), COLPF0/1/2 set to river-color scheme. This is where the bullet actually shows up.
comments.$b72a: DLI status bar: colors for score/fuel display area. HPOSP3 = dli_status_x ($27), then WSYNC loop advances HPOSP2/P3 together for the lives-icon display using color table $B48D.
comments.$b750: DLI status-bar tail: position player sprites, clear collision regs, fall through to XITVBV return.
comments.$b752: HPOSP3 write in the status-bar DLI, driven from dli_status_x ($27).
comments.$b773: JMP XITVBV — return from interrupt through the OS VBI exit.
comments.$b776: Display list template: copied to $3F00 at cold start ($A05D). 138 bytes including blank-lines, LMS instructions, and the JVB wrap.
comments.$b800: Sprite shape data ($B800-$BBFF): entity graphics, 1024 bytes of player/missile bitmaps for ships, helicopters, jets, fuel depots, balloons and bridges.
comments.$bab7: Fuel-gauge row pointer table: 2-byte pointers indexed by fuel_gauge_idx ($3B) via ASL/TAX at $AF0F-$AF18 (14 bytes through $BAC4).
comments.$bac5: Player color cycle table: 8 bytes through $BACC, cycled through to animate the player color.
comments.$bb0b: BCD score-value table per entity type (25 bytes through $BB23). Indexed by entity_type when crediting points at $A547.
comments.$bb24: Entity type transition table (12 bytes through $BB2F) - maps old entity type to next when recycling slots.
comments.$bb30: Sprite offset tables (48 bytes through $BB5F): per-entity offset into the shape data at $B800.
comments.$bb60: Sprite height tables (48 bytes through $BB8F): per-entity scanline span used by the DLI multiplexer.
comments.$bc00: Terrain bank shape templates (768 bytes through $BEFF): bank contours and obstacle masks for procedural river generation.
comments.$bffa: Vector table: cold=$A000 ($BFFA/$BFFB), warm=$8000 ($BFFC/$BFFD), IRQ=$A000 ($BFFE/$BFFF). Referenced by the relocator at $4086 for cold-start and IRQ setup.
comments.$005b_dli_use: Note: $5B is also used by the playfield DLI to compute HPOSM1 via the formula HPOSM1 = ($5B>>3)+$5C at $B520-$B52C. So $5B is dual-purpose: (1) the real fuel level (verified via cheat search 2026-04-14, see main $005b comment above), (2) input to the M1 bullet X position formula. M1's X happens to be derived from the fuel level — this is intentional aliasing or coincidence is TBD. The cheat-search confirmation that freezing $5B halts fuel drain is the authoritative source for the fuel role.
comments.$b54f: DLI baseline: HPOSP1 = $39. P1 is positioned each frame but its DMA buffer at $0D00-$0DFF is never written to — verified by write-watchpoint and 180-frame DMA sweep. The 'bullet' the game renders is M1 (drawn at $B14B), not P1.
comments.$b559: DLI baseline: HPOSP0 = $3C. $3C = 0 during gameplay, so this effectively hides P0 at X=0. The P0 DMA buffer ($0C00-$0CFF) is actively zeroed each frame via the generic indirect copier at $B012, so P0 renders blank even if it were on-screen. P0 is effectively unused in the playfield zone.
comments.$b14b: draw_bullet_pmg: writes the M1 at-rest template into missile DMA. M1 (bits 2-3 of packed missile DMA = $0C) is the muzzle/chambered bullet that always sits just above the jet. The routine sets X=$09 (iteration count) and Y=$09 (PAL) or Y=$10 (NTSC), then falls into the write loop at $B155 which writes 10 bytes of $0C into $0BCC,Y. EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED via bridge: with fire held, the M1 DMA band at $0BCC-$0BD5 stays present, AND a moving segment appears at lower addresses. **Update (2026-04-13):** M0 is NOT the in-flight bullet — M0 is the fuel bar indicator ($AEC8-$AEF9 draws M0 bits, COLPM0 color-cycled). The M0 motion loop at $A5CA only runs at fuel=0 (death effect). The in-flight bullet mechanism still needs full tracing — likely HPOSM1 position changes combined with terrain scroll, not bitmap movement.
comments.$b155: Bullet draw loop body: writes $0C (M1 bit pattern, bits 2-3) into missile DMA at $0BCC+Y. dey; dex; bpl back. X counts iterations (10 total), Y indexes into the M DMA buffer. Loop writes the at-rest bullet template; a separate mechanism (probably a second draw path keyed off bullet_alive state) emits the moving in-flight bullet at increasing scanline offsets.
comments.$b697: River-zone DLI: COLPM1 = $1E. This makes M1 (the bullet) visible at river scanlines — M1 inherits COLPM1 as its color even though P1 itself has no bitmap. The baseline COLPM1 = 0 store at $B541 keeps M1 hidden above the river.
comments.$aef7: Missile DMA M0-bit conditional store. Part of the routine in frame_sync.asm starting at $AEF1 that does `lda $0B00,X; and #$FC; ldy $C1; beq skip; ora #$03; sta $0B00,X` — i.e. read a missile DMA byte, clear its M0 bits, conditionally set them based on `$C1`, write back. Combined with the two preceding stores at $0AFE+X and $0AFF+X (which clear M0 bits without setting), this implements a moving M0 dot that trails a 2-byte clear behind it as `frame_ctr_copy` ($C2) advances. The original comment 'Missile DMA clear loop... wipes missile sprites' was wrong: this is NOT a wipe-all clear; it preserves M1/M2/M3 bits (`and #$FC`) and only manipulates M0. The actual M-DMA wipe (which clears all bit slices) lives in the `loc_B221` helper at $B221 — see that comment.
comments.$a116: SIZEM initialization: after the RAM copy loop at $A0FD (which wraps X from $FF to $00), `inx` makes X=$01, then `stx SIZEM` writes $01 to the SIZEM register ($D00C). This sets M0 to double width (bits 0-1=01) while M1/M2/M3 stay normal. Irrelevant in normal gameplay since M0 is dormant, but matters for any mod that repurposes M0.
comments.$a453: Horizontal joystick movement with acceleration. Reads joystick_dir ($19), checks bits 2-3 for left/right input. If neutral: resets velocity ($2B) and fraction ($58) to 0. If moving: increments $2B by 8 (capped at $F8), then does `$58 +/- $2B` — pixel movement (inc/dec player_sprite_x $57) occurs only on 8-bit overflow. This creates an inertia curve: ~8 frames to first pixel at minimum velocity, near-pixel-per-frame at max. Same system used vertically at $A499 with $59.
comments.$a1a4: Reads hardware TRIG0 ($D010) directly for the player fire button. Branches to $A1BF when fire is pressed (TRIG0 = 0). The game bypasses the OS STRIG0 shadow at $0284 — OS VBI is disabled because the game installs its own interrupt handlers.
comments.$aec8: Fuel bar COLPM0 color cycle: `lda frame_counter; lsr; lsr; lsr; and #$07; tax; lda player_color_cycle,X; sta COLPM0`. Cycles M0/P0 color through 8 yellow-to-green values ($1E,$2E,$3E,$4E,$6E,$7E,$9E,$BE) at 1/8 frame rate. This colors the M0 fuel bar in the status panel.
comments.$aed6: Fuel bar M0 bit draw: `ldx frame_ctr_copy; cpx #$1C; bcc skip`. If frame_ctr_copy >= $1C, clears M0 bits (and #$FC) at 3 consecutive missile DMA bytes, then conditionally sets M0 bits (ora #$03) at $0B00+X based on `$C1` (HPOSM0 source). When $C1 != 0, the fuel bar is visible; when $C1 = 0, the bar is hidden (empty fuel). The bar position ($C1 = HPOSM0) moves leftward as fuel depletes.
comments.$a40a: fuel_refuel_inc: Real fuel-refill code path (verified 2026-04-14). Reached from check_entity_collision when jet body ($1D) overlaps an entity of type > $0E (fuel depots are types $0F+). Code: `lda $5B; adc #$01; ldx #$04; bcc $A418; lda #$FF; sta $5C; ldx #$03`. Increments fuel by 1 per frame; on overflow ($5B was $FF) clamps to $FF and resets drain counter $5C. Sound type ($03 or $04) goes to X, then to $7A at $A41E to trigger the refuel tick sound.
comments.$a418: fuel_refuel_store: STA $5B inside the refuel path. THIS IS THE WRITE that increments the real fuel level. Setting a breakpoint here fires every frame while jet1 flies over a fuel depot — confirmed user-side 2026-04-14.
comments.$a41e: fuel_refuel_sound_set: STX $7A sets the engine_sound_param_0 to the refuel sound type ($03 or $04, depending on overflow path). The VBI sound dispatcher consumes $7A at $B2EE+ to play the distinctive refuel tick sound on a non-engine channel. Followed by `lda #$08; sta $7B` for sound duration.
comments.$a43f: fuel_drain_check: Every-frame fuel drain check inside check_entity_collision (no-collision fall-through path). Code: `lda $5C; sec; sbc #$20; bcs $A453; ldy $5B; bne $A451; lda #$02; ldy #$23; jmp $A43A`. Each frame, drain counter $5C - $20 is computed; if no borrow (BCS), $5C is updated and no fuel change. If borrow occurs, fuel ($5B) decrements by 1. When $5B reaches 0, JMP into play_score_sound with death sound params (A=$02, Y=$23).
comments.$a451: fuel_drain_dec: DEC $5B inside the drain path. THIS IS THE WRITE that decrements the real fuel level. Fires once per drain tick (every $20+ frames depending on $5C state).

notes: 2026-04-13: Fuel bar mechanism fully traced. M0 (Missile 0) serves dual purpose: (1) fuel bar indicator at $AEC8-$AEF9 — COLPM0 color-cycled through player_color_cycle ($1E-$BE = yellow-green), M0 bits drawn at $0B00+frame_ctr_copy gated by $C1 (HPOSM0 source = bar X position); (2) fuel-depletion death effect at $A5CA-$A607 (only active when fuel=0). The fuel gauge frame is ANTIC mode 6 custom chars 7-14 in COLPF3 (black), with PRIOR=$04 giving PF3 priority over M0. Status panel uses 3 mode-6 text lines at $1000-$103B with CHBASE=$08; color per character via bits 7-6 selecting COLPF0-3; background = COLBK. Score digit renderer at $B086 uses offset in $B1 to encode color: P1=$10/PF0, P2=$50/PF1, bridge=$90/PF2.

2026-04-13: Collision bytes $1A-$22 ONLY consumed by check_entity_collision ($A3E9) which ONLY runs at fuel_level=0. Fuel station entity type is $0F (not $07). Score lookup via $050B[slot] (shape index). player_death ($A688) only reached via lives_count ($2A) timer countdown, NOT enemy contact. Frame order: VBI → main_frame_sync → frame_update → attract_wait(blocks until DLI done) → state_dispatch → entity_dispatch → check_entity_collision.

2026-04-12: M0 motion loop at $A5CA gated by fuel_level=0. SIZEM=$01 at $A116 (M0 double width). Player movement uses velocity+fraction accumulation ($2B/$58). Main loop ~5.5 fps, VBI/DLI 50 fps. Bullet1 M1 template at $0BCC stationary — upward motion from HPOSM1 changes + scroll. Jet at P2 offset $AA (14 bytes). Free ZP $CC-$EF (36 bytes).

2026-04-11: Correction pass — M0 active (not cleared/unused), M2 is jet hit probe at jet_x+4, process_collision_results/check_terrain_collision are digit renderers not collision code, play_score_sound/update_lives_display are state-flag manipulators not sound/display routines, lives_count ($2A) is a death countdown timer not visible lives, player_death ($A688) is a 5-instruction hook not the real death handler, $C1 is M0 X position not terrain_draw_flag.
